title: mitrasacme secedens
intro: Mitrasacme secedens, also known as the desert broom or sticky broom, is a plant species that belongs to the family Mitrasacmeaceae. It is a shrub-like plant native to Australia, with sticky or hairy leaves and small white flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the indigenous Australian culture, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Mitrasacme secedens include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its health benefits.
